todaysEvents.summary: Project Freedom goes live May 4: US CENTCOM deploys guided-missile destroyers, 100+ aircraft, multi-domain unmanned platforms, 15,000 service members to Hormuz to 'restore freedom of navigation'; Trump: 'very positive discussions' with Iran; not a formal escort mission per US official - force authorized if Iran interferes (NBC News, PBS, Axios, May 3-4).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Largest US military commitment to Hormuz since crisis began - de-escalation in intent but military escalation in posture; Iran has not yet responded kinetically

todaysEvents.summary: Iran warns Project Freedom violates ceasefire: parliamentary security chief Azizi: any US interference in Hormuz maritime framework constitutes a ceasefire violation; Iranian FM Araghchi reviewing 14-point proposal vs US response; Trump: possibility US could restart Iran strikes (CNN, CNBC, May 3).
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Iran's explicit ceasefire-violation framing raises miscalculation risk; 15,000 US troops + destroyers in strait creates flashpoint; single incident would send Brent spiking

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $108.12 May 4 (0.0%, TradingEconomics; flat vs May 1 $108.10); TTF €45.08 (-2.3%, TradingEconomics); market interprets Project Freedom as ambiguous - partial transit signal vs escalation risk; Gold ~$4,621 est.; tail 45% unchanged (May 4).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Market not yet pricing Project Freedom as decisive; Hormuz physically still ~90% closed; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal; nuclear stalemate unchanged

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Project Freedom + Iran's 14-point proposal create a narrow path: if Iran stands down on Project Freedom (no kinetic response) and accepts nuclear addendum, vessels released, Hormuz reopens under mine-clearing protocol. Narrow (10%) - Trump 'not satisfied' with current 14-point proposal; nuclear stalemate persists; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal (PBS); full commercial reopening delayed even on deal. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 175bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Project Freedom creates partial transit capability alongside existing US blockade; Iran deters kinetic response given scale of US deployment (15,000 troops, destroyers, 100+ aircraft); 14-point nuclear negotiations stall; ships move partially under US escort. Brent stabilises $100-115/bbl; TTF €44-48/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 185-215bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (45%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Iran treats Project Freedom as ceasefire violation per Azizi's warning; IRGC fires on US guided-missile destroyers or aircraft entering Hormuz corridor; massive escalation locks in. Gulf-wide blockade tightens; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis; iTraxx Asia IG spikes to 230-260bp. US-Iran military confrontation in strait is the most likely trigger given 15,000 troops now in contact distance. Tail 45%.
